text.title: modern-ba-framework Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for modern-ba-framework as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Treating the "Modern Application" tables as procedures.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The tables are descriptive (e.g., "AI/ML literacy" under Analytical Thinking); they have no inputs, outputs, or acceptance tests. Agents that treat them as instructions produce hand-wavy plans.

failure-mode.description: Using certification tier (ECBA, CCBA, CBAP) as a capability proxy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Certs measure exam pass rate, not analyst quality with LLM tools, RAG, or evals; treat the cert ladder as procurement signaling, not skill assurance.

failure-mode.description: Applying SAFe BA framework in regulated industries (pharma, automotive, aerospace).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SAFe BA is not audit-grade; ASPICE, IEC 62304, DO-178C auditors require IREB or BABOK-aligned artifact vocabulary.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to BABOK because it is the most-tokenized BA reference in training data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force structured candidate scoring or you'll never see IREB / PMI-PBA / BCS picked. Without explicit scoring on all 8 frameworks, BABOK wins by default regardless of context fit.

failure-mode.description: Models confabulate clause numbers, KA boundaries, and IIBA version dates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin a fact-source file (cached BABOK ToC + BA Standard ToC + IREB CPRE syllabi) and require citations. Reject any structural claim not present in the cached files.

failure-mode.description: "BA Standard (2025)" wording will go stale; agent must check current IIBA publication titles before quoting versions to humans.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Update publication title checks when standards are released. Agents that quote outdated version numbers lose credibility in procurement.

failure-mode.description: The Agent Selection table is advisory. Cost regressions appear when the planner downgrades opus → sonnet for "gap analysis" or regulatory mapping.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock the model per task in the decision JSON. Do not let cost optimizations degrade output on critical signals like regulatory mapping.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-the-loop checkpoint is skipped; auto-execution bypasses the only place a real PO/BA can correct framework bias.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The mandatory checkpoint between selection and routing is where humans correct the default-BABOK pull. Never auto-execute through this gate.

failure-mode.description: Vocabulary glossary is sensitive: in M&A or vendor contexts it can leak commercial terms.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run password-scrubber-agent and tag the doc as confidential before persisting outside private repos.

failure-mode.description: Agent edits the README's tables; it tends to "modernize" them every run, churning git history.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confine edits to agent-integration.md and .aidocs/<feature>/. Pin the README tables as read-only.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-reference IREB, PMI-PBA, BCS, and Scrum.org product-owner tracks before presenting to the user. Single-vendor lists mislead procurement.

failure-mode.description: Agent quotes CMMI-DEV process areas as "framework" peers without flagging that CMMI is a maturity model, not a BA reference.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing maturity models and BA references confuses procurement. Keep them in separate decision tracks.

failure-mode.description: LLM defaults to BABOK because it dominates training data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force structured candidate scoring on all 8 frameworks; without it, IREB, PMI-PBA, and BCS are never selected regardless of context fit.

failure-mode.description: Agent confabulates clause numbers, KA boundaries, and IIBA version dates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin a local fact-source (cached BABOK ToC + IREB CPRE syllabi) and require citations to it; reject any structural claim not present in the cached files.

failure-mode.description: Agent auto-proceeds from selection to routing without a human checkpoint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The human-in-the-loop checkpoint between selection and routing is the only place a real BA can correct the framework bias introduced by training data; auto-execution removes this control.

failure-mode.description: Certification recommendations are IIBA-only, creating vendor bias.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-reference IREB, PMI-PBA, BCS, and Scrum.org product-owner tracks before presenting to the user; single-vendor cert lists mislead procurement.

failure-mode.description: Agent edits the framework README tables to "modernize" them on each run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confine edits to agent-integration.md and .aidocs/<feature>/; churning git history on the README tables destroys stable reference content.
